whatThisIs: Cross-reference the CDC's tetanus prophylaxis decision matrix: wound type (clean and minor vs. all other wounds) by immunization history (uncertain or fewer than three prior doses vs. three or more, and time since the last booster). The tile returns the recommended action - no vaccine, Td or Tdap booster, tetanus immune globulin (TIG), or both vaccine and TIG - and notes the CDC preference for Tdap when the patient has never received it, especially in pregnancy and household contacts of infants.
whenToUse: Use this in any ED, urgent-care, or primary-care visit for a wound, burn, or animal bite, and at routine adult and pregnancy visits where booster status is being reviewed. The dirty-wound criteria - contamination with dirt, feces, saliva, or soil; puncture wounds; avulsions; crush, burn, frostbite, or missile injuries - matter because they expand the TIG indication. A clean minor wound in a patient with three prior doses and a booster within the last 10 years needs nothing; a dirty wound in an undervaccinated patient may need both Tdap and TIG.
